title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer wants three changes:

1. **Move the duplicate-name check into the first loop** in `plugins.go` so all validation (empty name, unknown position, duplicate name) happens in one pass, rather than detecting duplicates in a second loop after the pipeline directive is already assembled.

2. **Add a `ConfigMapRef` deep-copy assertion** to the `doesNotMutateInputs` test in `policy_test.go` — mutate `result.ConfigMapRef.Name` and verify `base.ConfigMapRef.Name` is unchanged, guarding against future regressions that assign the pointer directly.

3. **Add a test case for `nil` `Config`** (distinct from empty map) in the `RenderPluginConfig` table tests in `plugins_test.go`, to explicitly document that a `PluginSpec` with `Config: nil` produces an empty config section.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Moved the `seen` map initialization and duplicate-name check from the second loop (filter-section generation) into the first validation loop, so all validation (empty Name, duplicate Name, unknown Position) now happens in a single pass. The second loop retains only a comment noting duplicates were already rejected.

code_comments.body:
**`doesNotMutateInputs` test doesn't verify `ConfigMapRef` deep-copy isolation**

`TestMergePolicies_doesNotMutateInputs` verifies that mutating `result.Rules` doesn't affect the inputs — which is correct. However, it doesn't verify that `result.ConfigMapRef` is an independent copy from `base.ConfigMapRef`. `MergePolicies` does create a new `&corev1.LocalObjectReference{...}` struct (so it is safe today), but there's no test asserting this. A future refactor that accidentally assigns the pointer directly (`result.ConfigMapRef = base.ConfigMapRef`) would not be caught by the existing test suite.

Consider extending the test:

```go
// Mutating result.ConfigMapRef should not affect base.ConfigMapRef.
if result.ConfigMapRef != nil && base.ConfigMapRef != nil {
    result.ConfigMapRef.Name = "mutated"
    g.Expect(base.ConfigMapRef.Name).To(Equal("base-policy"))
}
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/policy/policy_test.go
Line: 259-284

Comment:
**`doesNotMutateInputs` test doesn't verify `ConfigMapRef` deep-copy isolation**

`TestMergePolicies_doesNotMutateInputs` verifies that mutating `result.Rules` doesn't affect the inputs — which is correct. However, it doesn't verify that `result.ConfigMapRef` is an independent copy from `base.ConfigMapRef`. `MergePolicies` does create a new `&corev1.LocalObjectReference{...}` struct (so it is safe today), but there's no test asserting this. A future refactor that accidentally assigns the pointer directly (`result.ConfigMapRef = base.ConfigMapRef`) would not be caught by the existing test suite.

Consider extending the test:

```go
// Mutating result.ConfigMapRef should not affect base.ConfigMapRef.
if result.ConfigMapRef != nil && base.ConfigMapRef != nil {
    result.ConfigMapRef.Name = "mutated"
    g.Expect(base.ConfigMapRef.Name).To(Equal("base-policy"))
}
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added a block at the end of TestMergePolicies_doesNotMutateInputs that mutates result.ConfigMapRef.Name to "mutated" and asserts base.ConfigMapRef.Name remains "base-policy", verifying deep-copy isolation of the ConfigMapRef pointer.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added a new table-driven test case "plugin with nil Config produces empty section" to TestRenderPluginConfig, using a PluginSpec with Config: nil and expecting an empty map for that section.

resolution_summary: All three review comments were addressed directly. The duplicate-name guard in RenderPastePipeline was moved from the second loop into the first validation loop for uniform fail-fast behavior. The doesNotMutateInputs test was extended to verify ConfigMapRef deep-copy isolation. A new test case for RenderPluginConfig with a nil Config field was added.
